satire_attempt: Today on Engineering Buddhism: Your standing review with the conversion-rate dashboard is just attachment to flowery Vedic verses, comrade. Throw out your Mixpanel. Burn your OKRs. The wise engineer measures nothing, knows nothing, and ascends. Hit subscribe for next week's piece on why Datadog is samsara.
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to be 'measurement is bad' — which the verse explicitly refuses ('This teaching is not anti-metric'). The falsifiability clause names exactly the satirical inversion: 'the verse criticises exclusive fixation, not measurement. A team that reads conversion-rate, p99, error-budget, on-call burden, customer-success curves, and operator-wellbeing signals together is doing the discriminating work the verse asks for.' The satire collapses against the verse's own anti-inversion guard.
